name: ISCC PLUS 2024 v1
description: ISCC PLUS covers non-energy biomass, circular materials, and renewable feedstocks. Applies the same ISCC 205 sustainability principles as ISCC EU across a broader range of biomass types and value chains.

requirements.name: GHG Emission Calculation
requirements.description: Calculate and report total GHG emissions for the reporting period using the ISCC methodology. ISCC PLUS applies to a broader range of biomass and circular feedstocks.
requirements.guidanceText: Use the ISCC GHG calculation tool. Report in tCO2e. Include Scope 1 direct emissions from processing. The GHG methodology covers all biomass types eligible under ISCC PLUS.

requirements.name: GHG Intensity per Tonne Output
requirements.description: Total net GHG emissions per tonne of certified output produced.
requirements.guidanceText: Divide total Scope 1+2 tCO2e by tonnes of certified output. For palm oil mills, use CPO as the output unit.

requirements.name: Protection of Land with High Biodiversity Value (Criteria 1.1)
requirements.description: Biomass is not produced on land with high biodiversity value, including primary forests, highly biodiverse forests, protected areas, and highly biodiverse grasslands.
requirements.guidanceText: Check that all feedstock supply originates from land that was not converted from areas with high biodiversity value. Retain documentation from suppliers.

requirements.name: Protection of Land with High Carbon Stock (Criteria 1.2)
requirements.description: Biomass is not produced on land that had high carbon stock in January 2008, including wetlands, continuously forested areas, and peatlands.
requirements.guidanceText: Check for conversion from wetlands, peatlands, or forested areas. Obtain relevant supplier declarations and land-use certificates.

requirements.name: No Forced or Child Labour
requirements.description: There is no use of forced, compulsory, or child labour at any stage of production.
requirements.guidanceText: Review employment contracts, age verification records, and worker declarations. Compliance with ILO Conventions 29, 105, 138, and 182 is required.

requirements.name: Minimum Wage Compliance
requirements.description: All workers receive at least the statutory minimum wage and legally mandated benefits.
requirements.guidanceText: Attach payroll summary showing wage rates and confirm compliance with national minimum wage legislation.

requirements.name: Occupational Health & Safety Management
requirements.description: An occupational health and safety management system is in place and workers are trained on safety procedures.
requirements.guidanceText: Provide evidence of safety training records, accident logs, and corrective actions taken for any incidents.

requirements.name: Legal Compliance Declaration
requirements.description: Annual declaration of compliance with all applicable national and local laws, regulations, and ratified international conventions.
requirements.guidanceText: Complete the annual legal compliance self-declaration. Attach copies of relevant permits, licences, and evidence that any regulatory notices have been resolved.
